title: An Ancestor Forgiveness Ritual
subtitle: A twenty-minute ritual for the long pattern that came down through the family, not for one person to fix
excerpt: A twenty-minute ritual for the long pattern that came down through the family. Not the work of one person to fix. The ritual lets you set down the part of the pattern that does not need to keep moving forward through you.
sourceNotes: Original to homemade.education. The ancestor work has many lineages across faith and across cultural custom.

practice.whenToUse: Use when you have named a long pattern in the family that is not yours to keep carrying. Especially good on the night before a family visit or after a hard conversation across generations.

content.text: A twenty-minute ritual for the long pattern that came down through the family. The ritual is not about fixing one person. It is about choosing what you carry forward and what you set down.
New to ritual as a practice? Read
how rituals work
first.
What you will need
A candle. A glass of water. A piece of paper and a pen. Twenty minutes of quiet.
The practice
Prepare

Sit somewhere quiet. Place the candle, the water, and the paper in front of you. Light the candle. Bring to mind, gently, the long pattern you have been carrying. Money fear, anger, silence, scarcity, harshness, whatever it is.
Release (repeat x3)
I am ready to release the parts of this pattern that are not mine to carry. I release them now. I release them now. I release them now.
Allow (repeat x3)
I am ready to allow a different shape to live through me. I allow it now. I allow it now. I allow it now.
Integrate
On the paper, write three short lines. The first names the pattern as you have known it. The second names what it cost the people who carried it before you. The third names what you are setting down.
Read the three lines out loud, slow. Then say one sentence of gratitude for what the family did carry well, even with the pattern in the room.
Ground it
I am of them. I am also my own.
Drink the glass of water slowly. Blow the candle out. Keep the paper somewhere safe for the next month. The setting-down is the work; the keeping-of-the-paper is the witness.
Where this practice comes from
Original to homemade.education. Ancestor work has many lineages in faith and in cultural custom. The version here uses no closed-tradition framing.
